rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical iodide-mediated intramolecular selenocyclization of unsaturated alcohols (and acids) with diaryl diselenides to give selenomethyl-substituted cyclic ethers and lactones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of iodide (I-) to I+ (via 2e- loss), which acts as electrophilic mediator generating an iodonium ion on the alkene; cathode reduces I2 back to I- and protons to H2, closing the redox cycle

key_intermediates.name: iodo-cyclic ether intermediate B (after intramolecular O-attack and deprotonation)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes I- to I+ (2e- loss); I+ activates the alkene of the unsaturated alcohol to form an iodonium cation (A), which undergoes intramolecular O-cyclization with loss of H+ to give iodo-ether B; B is displaced by PhSe- (from PhSeSePh, releasing 1/2 I2) to deliver the selenoether product, and the cathode reduces I2 to I- and H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3b obtained in 75% yield under standard conditions; no inhibition, arguing against a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3b obtained in 74% yield; no inhibition, arguing against a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: I2 (no current)
probe_reagents.observation: With I2 (1 equiv) and no current, 3b formed in 50% yield, supporting I+ as the active electrophilic species

probe_reagents.name: NH4I without current
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction between 1b and 2a with NH4I in the absence of current — confirms electrochemical activation needed

probe_reagents.name: divided cell experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Only trace 3b in divided cell, proving cathodic reduction of I2 back to I- is essential for catalytic turnover

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of NH4I, 1a, 2a and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: NH4I shows two oxidation peaks at 0.38 V (I-/I2) and 0.67 V (I3-/I2); curves unchanged upon addition of 1a or 2a, indicating I- is oxidized first and substrates are not directly oxidized

notes: A radical mechanism was explicitly considered but excluded based on TEMPO/BHT non-inhibition and the I2-only control giving product. The divided-cell experiment establishes paired electrolysis: anodic I- oxidation coupled with cathodic I2/H+ reduction is required for catalytic turnover.
